sections.paragraph: m. Ch. pr. rolling; hence weight, magnitude (see the root, note under Kal), Ezr 5:8 6:4 , אֶבֶן גְּלָל “great, heavy, squared stones,” such as were rolled, not carried. In German, a book of large size, such as might be rolled sooner than carried, according to the present custom, in a bag, is called jocosely ein Wälzer. Talm. גללא without אבן is used of a large stone (Buxt. page 433).
